functional_requirements.description: User can create an account using email and password.
User must verify email before first login.
System sends a secure URL containing a cryptographically secure, high-entropy random token as a query parameter for email verification.
System displays message 'The verification link is invalid or has expired. Please request a new one.' when verification link is expired or token is invalid.
System redirects user to a page where they can trigger a new verification email when link is expired or token is invalid.
System displays message 'An unexpected error occurred. Please try again later.' on system error during verification.
System provides a 'Resend Verification Email' button on the landing page when verification link is expired.
System automatically purges unverified user records from the database after 7 days.

When a user attempts to create an account with an email that already exists but is not yet verified, the system updates the existing unverified record with the new password hash and generates a brand-new verification token, sending it to the email address, resetting the 24-hour expiration clock.

business_rules.description: Password must contain at least one uppercase letter.
Password must contain at least one lowercase letter.
Password must contain at least one numeric digit.
Password must contain at least one special character (e.g., @, #, $, %, ^, &, *).
Consecutive spaces are forbidden in the password.
Password cannot contain the user's explicit email prefix or username.
Email verification link expires exactly 24 hours after issuance.
Tokens are hashed (using SHA-256) before storage in the database.
All verification traffic must be strictly enforced over HTTPS (TLS 1.3).
In all failure cases, raw database or system error details must be hidden from the frontend to prevent information disclosure.

When a user attempts to create an account with an email that already exists but is not yet verified, the registration flow proceeds normally from the frontend perspective to prevent email enumeration attacks.

validations.description: Password length must be between 8 and 12 characters.
Password must contain at least one uppercase letter.
Password must contain at least one lowercase letter.
Password must contain at least one numeric digit.
Password must contain at least one special character (e.g., @, #, $, %, ^, &, *).
Password must not contain consecutive spaces.
Password must not contain the user's explicit email prefix or username.
Verification token must be validated as single-use; once clicked or validated, the token is immediately invalidated in the database.
Verification link must be checked for expiration (24 hours from issuance).

dependencies.description: Email delivery service is required to send verification emails.
Database is required to store user records, password hashes, and verification tokens.
HTTPS/TLS 1.3 must be enforced on the server for all verification traffic.
Cryptographic library is required to generate high-entropy random tokens and perform SHA-256 hashing.

risks.description: Email delivery failures could prevent users from completing verification, leading to poor user experience and potential account lockout.
If the token generation or hashing implementation is flawed, security could be compromised (e.g., token prediction, collision).
The 7-day purge of unverified records may conflict with user expectations if they attempt to verify after the purge but before re-registering.
The behavior of updating an existing unverified record instead of creating a duplicate could lead to confusion if the user is not notified of the update.

missing_information.description: The exact landing page URL or route for the 'Resend Verification Email' button is not specified.
The format or content of the verification email (e.g., sender name, subject line, body text) is not specified.
The behavior when a user attempts to login without verifying their email is not specified (e.g., error message, redirect).
The maximum number of resend verification email requests allowed within a time period is not specified.
The process for requesting a new verification email (e.g., endpoint, rate limiting) is not detailed.

requirement_items.description: User can create an account using email and password.
User must verify email before first login.
System sends a secure URL containing a cryptographically secure, high-entropy random token as a query parameter for email verification.
System displays message 'The verification link is invalid or has expired. Please request a new one.' when verification link is expired or token is invalid.

System provides a 'Resend Verification Email' button on the landing page when verification link is expired.
System automatically purges unverified user records from the database after 7 days.

When a user attempts to create an account with an email that already exists but is not yet verified, the system updates the existing unverified record with the new password hash and generates a brand-new verification token, sending it to the email address, resetting the 24-hour expiration clock.
Email must be unique.
Password minimum length is 8 characters.
Password maximum length is 12 characters.
Password must contain at least one uppercase letter.
Password must contain at least one lowercase letter.
Password must contain at least one numeric digit.
Password must contain at least one special character (e.g., @, #, $, %, ^, &, *).
Consecutive spaces are forbidden in the password.
Password cannot contain the user's explicit email prefix or username.
Email verification link expires exactly 24 hours after issuance.
Verification tokens are single-use only.
Tokens are hashed (using SHA-256) before storage in the database.
All verification traffic must be strictly enforced over HTTPS (TLS 1.3).
In all failure cases, raw database or system error details must be hidden from the frontend to prevent information disclosure.

When a user attempts to create an account with an email that already exists but is not yet verified, the registration flow proceeds normally from the frontend perspective to prevent email enumeration attacks.
Email must be unique.
Password length must be between 8 and 12 characters.
Password must contain at least one uppercase letter.

Password must contain at least one numeric digit.
Password must contain at least one special character (e.g., @, #, $, %, ^, &, *).

Password must not contain the user's explicit email prefix or username.

Email delivery service is required to send verification emails.
Database is required to store user records, password hashes, and verification tokens.
HTTPS/TLS 1.3 must be enforced on the server for all verification traffic.
Cryptographic library is required to generate high-entropy random tokens and perform SHA-256 hashing.
